isolation.notes: All project commands executed via nix develop -c ...; the compile used DUNE_BUILD_DIR inside the evidence directory so validation did not touch the shared _build/ tree.

toolsUsed: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR="/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/dune-build" dune exec rgoc -- "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg" -o "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go"

assertions.title: Stdlib call sites use snake_case while stdlib type names stay PascalCase

steps.action: Compile a representative fixture that uses use net::http, http::listen_and_serve, http::new_serve_mux, mux.handle_func, http::Request, http::ResponseWriter, and http::ServeMux.
steps.expected: rgoc exits 0, writes generated Go, and leaves the flow ready for gofmt -d, go build, and go vet.
steps.observed: rgoc exited 1 with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux` at stdlib_cross_bridge.rg:9:9, so no generated Go file was emitted.

steps.action: Check the requested -o path after the failed compile.
steps.expected: No misleading output file remains only if compilation failed before code generation.
steps.observed: outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go did not exist after the command (`output_exists=false`).

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR="/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/dune-build" dune exec rgoc -- "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg" -o "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go"

if [ -e "/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go" ]; then echo "output_exists=true"; else echo "output_exists=false"; fi
diagnostics: error: /home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/evidence/import-surface/import-surface-cli-round2/fixtures/stdlib_cross_bridge.rg:9:9: no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux

assertions.issues: The naming bridge still fails at the receiver method step: mux.handle_func(...) is rejected before any generated Go can prove the snake_case-to-Go lowering.

assertions.title: Import, naming, and stdlib type usage compose coherently in one program

steps.action: Reuse the same representative fixture so one program imports net::http, uses snake_case stdlib call names, and mentions PascalCase stdlib types in the same file.
steps.expected: The combined flow compiles successfully and yields generated Go containing the lowered net/http spellings.
steps.observed: The combined program failed at compile time with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux` at stdlib_cross_bridge.rg:9:9, preventing generated-Go inspection.

steps.action: Check whether the requested generated-Go output exists after the failed combined-flow compile.
steps.expected: A failed combined-flow compile leaves no misleading output file behind.
steps.observed: outputs/stdlib_cross_bridge.go did not exist after the command (`output_exists=false`).

assertions.issues: The combined import+naming+type flow still breaks on mux.handle_func(...), so the full single-program composition contract is not yet satisfied.

frictions.description: Each nix develop invocation prepended the flake shell banner and parser-generator warnings before the actual rgoc diagnostic, adding log noise during CLI inspection.
frictions.resolution: Captured the full compile log plus a terminal snapshot so the final user-facing diagnostic and output-existence check remained explicit.

summary: Retested 2 previously failing import-surface assertions. Both still fail because the representative fixture is rejected with `no method 'handle_func' on type http::ServeMux`, and the requested generated-Go output file is not created.
